Mujh ko bāwarchī yoṅ dhirāte haiṅ, Rah, terī āsh kyā pakāte haiṅ! Saudā. (A satire on a miser whose cook, with an empty larder, threatens to roast the starved guest who calls for dinner). 2. To conspire or plot against. āsh pulāo , n. f. A pulāo of ground barley given to patients. Mȳāṅ ko bulāo, khilāo āsh pulāo . Nursery Song. āsh jau , n. m. Barley water; gruel.

ety: act. part. of عشق 'to love passionately,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Loving passionately, amorous, enamoured, in love; — a passionate lover, a lover, an inamorato
more: n. m. عشق loved excessively. 1. A lover; paramour; amateur. 1. Āshaq to qalandar haiṅ, na Hindū, na Musalmān . Nazīr. The lover is a devotee, nor Musalman nor Hindu he. 2. Āshaq ko Khudā zar de, nahīṅ kar de zamīṅ parde . Prov. The lover Heaven bless with coin or to earth's bosom him resign! 3. Āshiq chūhā, bhaiṅs padamnī, meṅḍak tāl lagāve, Cholī pahre gadahā nāche, ūṅṭ bishanpad gāve . The times out of joint. Kabīr. Rats lovers grown do buffalos woo, and frogs play and keep time, The Ass see dancing in a coat, the camel hymns doth chime! 4. Jab tak banī, banī, na banī ghar kī rāh lī, Ashiq haiṅ, yā Shahīdī, kisī ke gulām haiṅ? While we agree we agree, if not I'll homeward go, A lover or a martyr I, but slave—oh no! 2. Suitor; wooer; adorer. 3. A careless, reckless person. āshiq-mizāj . Sportive; sprightly; gay; merry; jolly. āshiq o māshūq , 1. Lovers. 2. A snake belt. āshaq h. , v. n.…
part. act. (of عشق) used substantively, A lover. (Pers. plur. عاشقان). عاشق هونا āﻌshiḳ honā, To fall in love.
subs: adj. 'Having the temper or disposition of a lover,' sprightly, sportive, gay, merry
Lover and beloved, lovers; — a snake-belt
(-par), To be enamoured (of), to be, or to fall, in love (with), to admire.
wety: First attested in c. 1503 as Middle Hindi عاشِق ('aśq), borrowed from Classical Persian عَاشِق (āšiq), from Arabic عَاشِق (ʕāšiq).

def: s.f. Making love, amorousness, love, courtship; gallantry; the being in love, the state or condition of a lover

s. f. Making love, amour, love, courtship, gallantry, the state of being a lover.

def: adj. Acquainted (with, -se), knowing, known; attached (to), fond (of)
more: n. 1. Friend; companion; acquaintance; crony; intimate friend; bosom friend; familiar. 2. A lover; mate; paramour; sweetheart; cavalier servente; cicisbeo; flame; beau; amoroso. Ranḍī ke saikṛoṅ āshnā . Prov. A harlot's lovers may be reckoned by the hundred. 3. A kept mistress; inamorata ; concubine. Jin kī āshnā, unkī bagal meṅ . Prov. saying. 4. A woman's dog (a lover). 5. ( asnāo ). Rus. A son-in-law. āsh'nā , adj. 1. Acquainted; knowing. Maiṅ un se āshnā bhī nahīṅ . 2. Fond of; attached to; loving. Ranḍī paise kī āshnā . Prov. Money is the harlot's love. harf āsh'nā , n. An abecedarian; a freshwater sailor. dost āshnā, yār āshnā ; Rus. yār asnāo , n. m. Friends; relatives and kinsmen. sūrat āshnā , n. m. One known by sight only; a formal acquaintance.
s. m. f. An acquaintance, lover, friend. آشنا پرست āshnā-parast, Friendly, attentive to friends, sociable.
subs: adj. Friendly, attentive to friends, sociable, obliging
s.m.f. An apparent or outward friend
v.n. To become very friendly; to become tame or domesticated (an animal; syn. hil-jānā).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آشْنَا (āšnā).

def: s.f. Acquaintance, friendship, intimacy, familiarity; connection, relationship; connection by marriage; illicit love, carnal intercourse
more: n. f. 1. Friendship; intimacy; familiarity; acquaintance; intercourse. 2. Relationship; connexion; relation through marriage. Mhārī unkī asnāī hai . Rus. 3. Carnal knowledge; illicit love; unlawful attachment. Dekho, bhaṛwe kī shāmat āī hai, Mujh se kahtā hai, "āshnāī hai ." Wom. āshnāī kar'nā, āshnāī joṛnā,—lagānā , v. a. 1. To form a friendship or intimacy; make friends with. Āshnāī karnī āsān, nibhānā mushkil . Prov. Friendships are soon made, but rarely ripened. 2. To form an unlawful attachment or connection. Rāt kisī ke saṅg tū jāgī, kar gaī chatrāī, Merī jān, is bāman ke saṅg karī āshnāī . Song. Lāonī. āshnāī lagnā, āshnāī honā , v. n. To become friends; to be united in friendship; to be fast friends.
s. f. Acquaintance, friendship. آشناٴي کرنا āshnā-ī-karnā, v. a. To associate, to be familiar, to unite. آشنائي لگنا āshnā-ī lagnā, v. n. To become intimate, to be united in friendship.
subs: āshnāʼī karnā (-se), To make acquaintance (with), make friends (with), strike up a friendship (with); to form an attachment or an intimacy (with); to form an unlawful attachment or connection (with)
āshnāʼī honā, v.n. To become friends (with), become intimate (with), to be united in friendship (with).

def: s.m. Tumult, clamour; storm, tempest; terror; misfortune; inflammation of the eyes; an impostor; — part. adj. (used in comp.) exciting.
more: s. m. 1. Tumult, clamour. 2. Terrour. 3. Misfortune. 4. Storm, tempest. 5. An impostor. آشوبگهہ āshob-gah, Place of tumult, &c.

def: n. m. from asok, Jonesia asoca , Wat., and ashṭmī , eighth of the light fortnight of Chait , (April-May) when a festival in honor of Vishnu is observed, part of the ceremonial of which consists in drinking water with the buds of asoka in it.
adj. f. (m. ʻasharat; see ʻashara), Ten.
more: n. m. A tenth; a tithe. In Mah. law, the tithe assessed on lands occupied by Mahomedans, or which have been taken from infidels and divided amongst a victorious army. ushr-i-ashīr ; Pop. ashr-i-ashīr ; H. savāṅ , adj. 1. A hundredth part. 2. Very little.

def: (for sharābāt), s.f. pl. Drinks prohibited by the Moḥammadan law, such as wines, &c. (see sharāb).
adj. Well-born, of good family, noble; gentle, meek, mild; refined, courteous, urbane.
more: n. m. شَرُفَ he became eminent. pl. of. شريف 1. Nobles; noblemen; persons of rank; grandees; gentlemen; men of high extraction; gentry; gentle folk; squirearchy; magnates. 2. Preux chevaliers ; honorable men. 3. In Rohilkhund, Oude, and Benares, a class of cultivators who claim certain privileges. ashrāf , adj. H. bhalā-mānas . 1 Gentle; meek; mild. 2. Refined; courteous; urbane; wellmannered.
s. plur. (of شریف) Nobles, grandees, gentlemen, men of high extraction.
(inf. iv. of شرف) s. Ennobling, exaltation.
wety: From Arabic أَشْرَاف (ʔašrāf).

def: s.m. A person of noble birth, nobleman; gentleman
more: adj. compar. (of شریف) More or most noble, most eminent. s. m. A gentleman, a person of noble birth, a nobleman. اشرف المخلوقات ashrafu-l-maḵẖlūḳāt, The most excellent of created beings; mankind. اشرف المکانات ashrafu-l-makānāt, The most eminent of places.
subs: The noblest or most excellent of created things, mankind

ety: abst. subst. from ashrāf, and = A. sharāfat, q.v.
def: s.f. Nobility, nobleness; gentleness, affability, urbanity, civility, courtesy, good-breeding, politeness.
more: n. f. H. bhal-mansāt . 1. Gentleness; affability; amiablity; obligingness. 2. Civility; urbanity; courtesy. 3. Politeness; good breeding; good manners.

ety: said to be so called from Ashraf, the king in whose reign the coin obtained currency
def: s.f. Name of a gold coin, a gold-mohar; (the value of a Calcutta gold-mohar is about 16 rupees).
more: A gold coin; a gold mohar, equivalent to about 16 rupees. Ghar meṅ ek kauṛī nāhīṅ, nāoṅ Asharfī Lāl . Prov. Not a kauṛī in the house, and he is called Asharfī Lāl .

s. f. A gold coin so called. (The Calcutta ashrafī is worth ₤1. 11 s. 8 d. and the gold of it is better than English standard gold by five shillings in the ounce, or about 1/16. By the regulations of May 1793, it should weigh 190.894 grains troy).

def: adj. & s.m. Wicked, wretched, criminal, seditious; — the wicked, &c.
more: s. plur. (of شریر) Wicked, wretched, criminal, seditious (people).

def: n. m. lit . The eight metals. 1. Bell metal; mixed metal. 2. Fac . A son born in adultery.

n. m. 1. A horse with a white face, tail, breast and hoofs. 2. A collection of eight lucky objects to be got together on occasion, as a lion, a bull, an elephant, a water-jar, a fan, a flag, a trumpet, and a lamp.

n. m. 1. The eight siddhis (perfect or superior beings). 2. A personification of the powers and laws of nature, when they are subjected to the will by holiness and austerities. Whatever the fancy desires, may, it is said, be obtained; universal sovereignty may be acquired, and implicit obedience to any command enforced; the magnitude, weight, or levity of the body may be increased or diminished at will, and the body be transported in an instant to any part of the universe.
adj. Eight ( = H. āṭh)
more: part. Desired, approved, reverenced; adored, respected, beloved. s. m. A god, a deity, an object, a beloved person, any thing wished for.
subs: (S. ashṭan+anga), s.m. Eight parts of the body with which very profound obeisance is performed (viz. the hands, thighs, breast, eyes, and forehead); — adj. With, or consisting of, eight members or parts
(ashṭāng-parnām), s.m. Prostration in adoration or salutation by which the ground is touched with the eight principal parts of the body
adj. Possessed of, or having, eight arms
( = āṭhoṅ pahar), ashṭa-jām, adv. Through the eight watches, night and day, always, incessantly
s.m. The eight metals; bell-metal, mixed metal ( = azhdhāt)
s.m. Anything consisting of a combination of the eight metals
ashṭa-dhāvī, s.f. A compound of the eight metals, &c. ( = ashṭa-dhātu)

s.m. The eight siddhis or superior beings, personifications of the powers and laws of nature: (when those powers are subjected to the will by holiness and austerity, whatever the fancy desires may, it is said, be obtained; even universal sovereignty may be acquired and implicit obedience to any command enforced; the magnitude or weight of the body may be increased or diminished at will, or the body transferred in an instant to any part of the universe)
s.m. Lord of the eight worlds

s.m. A norse with a white face, tail, breast, and hoofs; a collection of eight lucky things to be assembled on certain occasions (as a lion, a bull, an elephant, a water-jar, a fan, a flag, a trumpet and a lamp; or a Brahman, a cow, fire, gold, ghee, the sun, water, and a king).

def: adj. Forty-eight.
adj. Eighteenth.
s. m. The eight metals, said to be gold, silver, copper, brass, tin, bell-metal, lead, and iron.
adj. Of, or made of, the eight metals enumerated under اشٿدهات ash̤ṭadhāt.
s.m. One eighth; — adj. Eight-fold.
adj. Eighth; — s.m. An eighth.
s.f. The eighth day (night) in a (lunar) half-month. (See janam-ashṭamī.)
more: s. f. The eighth day of the lunar fortnight.

def: adj. Ninety-eight.

n. m. The eight members of the body. ashṭāṅg pranām,—parnām, sāshṭaṅg daṅḍvat . n. m. Prostration or adoration so as to touch the ground with the eight principal members, viz., with the hands, thighs, breast, eyes, and head. ashṭ jām , H. āṭhoṅ pahar , adv. lit . Eight watches of the day. Always; night and day; every moment. Ashṭ jām dhyān mohe wā ko rahat hai rī, Nā jānūṅ kab darshan paiṭhūṅgī . E. Song. Khayāl .

Eight parts of man, viz. the hands, feet, thighs, breast, eyes, head, words and mind. اشٿانگ پرنام ash̤ṭāng-praṇām, s. m. Prostration, in salutation or adoration, so as to touch the ground with those eight parts.
adj. Fifty-eight.
adj. Seventy-eight.
adj. Sixty-eight.
adj. Eighty eight.

The eight siddhi, the name of a superiour order of beings, a personification of the powers and laws of nature. (When they are subjected to the will by holiness and austerities, whatever the fancy desires may be obtained; universal sovereignty may be acquired, and implicit obedience to any commands enforced; the magnitude, weight, or levity of the body may be increased or diminished to an indefinite degree; and, it may be rendered invisible and transported in an instant to any part of the universe).
adj. Thirty-eight.
= آفتابد āftāba, q.v.
adj. Twenty-eight.
adj. Twenty-eighth.

ety: Pehl. āshtis, āshtia, Zend ākhsti, rt. śtā = S. स्था
def: s.f. Peace, concord, reconciliation, agreement, accommodation, convention, confederacy; amour, intrigue
more: s. f. 1. Peace, concord, reconciliation, accommodation, agreement, convention, confederacy. 2. Amour, intrigue.

def: = اشٿهي ashṭhī, q.v.
n. f. The eighth day of the increase or decrease of the moon. janam ashṭ'mī , n. f. The eighth day of the dark half of Bhādoṅ (August); the birthday of Krishna .
adj. See اسده a-suddh.

ety: perf. part. of āshuftan; ā+rt. Zend. khshub = S. kshub
def: part. adj. Distracted, disturbed, distressed; disordered; uneasy, wretched, miserable; enamoured, deeply in love
more: adj. Distracted, distressed, disturbed, disordered, wretched, miserable, uneasy. آشفته حال āshufta-ḥāl, Distressed in condition. آشفته حالي āshufta-ḥāl-ī, s. f. Distress of condition. آشفته خاطر āshufta-ḵẖāt̤ir, or آشفته دل āshufta-dil, Afflicted in mind. آشفته دماغي āshufta-dimāg̠ẖī, or آشفته سري āshufta-sarī, s. f. Distraction of mind or of the senses. آشفته زلف āshufta-zulf, or آشفته مو āshufta-mū, With dishevelled hair, shaggy. آشفته طبع āshufta-t̤abﻌ, Distracted, troubled in mind. آشفته طبعي āshufta-t̤abʼīﻌ, s. f. Distraction or trouble of mind.
subs: adj. Distressed in condition, embarrassed; distracted, &c. ( = āshufta)
s.f. Distress of condition, embarrassment; state of distraction, wretchedness, &c.
āshufta-dil, adj. Distressed or afflicted in mind, disquieted, uneasy
āshufta-sarī, s.f. Distraction of mind, or of the senses, derangement of the mind, madness
āshufta-mū, adj. With disordered or dishevelled hair; shaggy
āshufta-damāg̠, āshufta-sar, adj. Distracted in mind, head or brain

def: s.f. Distraction, perturbation, uneasiness; misery.
more: s. f. Distraction, perturbation, uneasiness, misery.

def: s.m. The tenth day, or the first ten days, of the month Moharram
subs: s.m. A temporary structure for the celebration of the Moḥarram festival; a house for the reception of the banners, &c. used in the Moḥarram procession.

def: s.m. = ʻāshūra, q.v. s.m. The tenth day, or the first ten days, of the month Moharram
subs: s.m. A temporary structure for the celebration of the Moḥarram festival; a house for the reception of the banners, &c. used in the Moḥarram procession.

def: s.m. The tenth day, or the first ten days, of the month Moharram
more: (from عشر Heb. ) s. m. The first ten days of the Muḥarram.
subs: s.m. A temporary structure for the celebration of the Moḥarram festival; a house for the reception of the banners, &c. used in the Moḥarram procession.

ety: for A. عشوة, v.n. fr. عشو; see ʻishā, and ʻashā
def: s.m. Darkness, the first of the darkness (of the night); — a confused and dubious affair, or venture, or undertaking, an obscure or concealed affair; a dangerous affair; — a fire seen from a distance at night; a beacon; — (met.) coquetry, ogling, blandishments, amorous playfulness
more: Poet. n. m. Ogling. ashva-gar , Poet. n. m. 1. One who ogles. 2. A coquette; wanton.

(from عشا To be covered, hidden, darkened, blind at night) s. m. 1. An obscure, concealed, dangerous affair. 2. The beginning of darkness at night. 3. A fire seen from a distance at night. 4. ( met. but most used in Persian and Hindī) Coquetry, ogling, blandishments, amorous playfulness. عشوه گر aﻌshwa-gar, A coquette. عشوه گري aﻌshwa-gar-ī, s. f. Coquetry.
subs: s.m. & f. One who ogles; a coquette

def: n. m. S. अश्व a horse, मेधृ to kill. The actual or emblematic sacrifice of a horse, an ancient religious ceremony among the Hindūs. ☞ This sacrifice is one of the highest order, and its performance a hundred times, gives a title to the dominion of Swarga or paradise. It appears to have been originally typical, the horse and other animals being simply bound during the performance of certain ceremonies. The actual sacrifice is an introduction of a later period. Wilson.
more: s. m. The ceremony of sacrificing a horse.

def: s. m. A person of rank attended by horsemen, a horseman.
s.f. pl. (of شي shai), Things, effects, chattels.
more: n. f. pl. of شي Things; effects; chattels.

def: s.m. Sword, scimitar. (Syn. کهڙگ khaṛg)
subs: s.m. Blade of a sword or scimitar; scabbard; a hell paved with swords; a sugar-cane.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀏𑀲𑁄 (eso), from Sanskrit एषः (eṣaḥ).

ety: for A. عاصٍ ʻāṣiṉ, act. part. of عصي 'to disobey, to rebel'
def: adj. & s.m. Disobedient, rebellious; criminal; — a rebel; a sinner.
more: (part. act. of عصي) 1. adj. Disobedient, criminal, rebellious. 2. s. m. and f. A rebel, a sinner.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عاصی ('āsī), from Arabic عَاصٍ (ʕāṣin).

def: = اس asi, q.v. s.m. Sword, scimitar. (Syn. کهڙگ khaṛg)
more: adj. Liar, untrue. ¶ असती (from अ and सती Virtuous wife) a-satī, adj. Disloyal or unchaste (a wife).
s. m. A sword, a scimitar.
pron. objective case sing. of وه wuh, q. v.
subs: s.m. Blade of a sword or scimitar; scabbard; a hell paved with swords; a sugar-cane.

ety: अ+H. सीचा, perf. part. of सीचना; S. rt. सिच्
def: adj. Not watered, not irrigated.
adj. Not effected, unaccomplished, imperfect, incomplete; unripe, raw, not properly cooked; invalid; false.
s.m. Blessing, benediction; admonition, instruction.
more: s. m. 1. A blessing, benediction. 2. Instruction.

def: part. adj. Untaught, illiterate, ignorant.
adj. Ill-behaved; bad-natured.

adj. Of good stock, lineage or pedigree, of good family, well-born, noble; of pure breed; game (cock); gentle, free from vice; high-spirited; — s.f. A maid-servant who is free (in contr. to launḍī a 'purchased slave').
more: adj. 1. Gentle; free from vice; quiet. Ghoṛā baṛā asīl hai . 2. High spirited; high mettled; brave. Asīl jānwar mūṅh nahīṅ phertā . 3. Well-born; noble; of good family; of high caste; of noble origin. Asīl kī murgī ṭake ṭake! Prov. A penny for a game bird! (Said of unappreciated merit.) 4. ( Ironic .) Low born; of low origin or caste. asīl ṭaṭ'ṭū , n. m. lit . A quiet pony. One possessing self-control; a virtuous or moral man. Asīl ṭaṭṭū sau ghoṛyoṅ meṅ bhī kān nahīṅ hilātā. asīl mur/?/g , n. m. A game cock.
Mah. n. f. A maid servant, who is a free woman, and therefore superior to the launḍī or purchased slave.
adj. Well born, noble, genuine: (in Hind.) s. f. A maidservant.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic أَصِيل (ʔaṣīl).

def: adj. & s.m. Sinning, doing what is unlawful; sinner, criminal; — a great and habitual liar.
adj. Sinful; sinner.

ety: act. part. of عصم 'to protect, or defend,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Defending, protecting; protected, safe; virtuous, chaste; — a defender, &c.
more: (part. act. of عصم) adj. Virtuous, chaste, protected, safe. ابو عاصم abū āﻌṣim (father of the safe or defended) i. e. Barley broth.
subs: 'Father-safe,' an appellation of the meal of parched barley or the like, and also of barley broth.

def: adj. Without limits or boundaries; unlimited, unbounded, boundless.
adj. Amazed, astonished, bewildered, confused, confounded (e.g. sar-āsīma, bewildered in the head, astounded).

ety: for A. عاصمة, fem. of ʻāṣim
def: adj. & s.f. Virtuous, chaste; — a chaste woman.
more: adj. f. (of عاصم q. v.) Chaste.

def: s.m. The sixth month of the Hindūs (Sept.-Oct.), during which the moon is full near the stars at the head of Aries.
more: W. n. m. S. आश्विनः from अश्विनी the first of the twenty-eight constellations in the moon's path. The seventh month of the Hindū luni-solar year (Sept.-Oct.) when the moon is full, near the stars in the head of Aries. Āsin kā mahīnā hai, jāṛe kī āmad hai . September's come, the harbinger of winter's come.
s. m. The sixth Hindū month, in which the moon is full near the stars in the head of Aries. September-October.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit आसन (āsana).

def: adj. Seated, sitting.
adj. = اسيچا asīćā, q.v. adj. Not watered, not irrigated.
more: adj. Not watered, not irrigated (a field, &c.).

def: adj. Marking; marked, favoured; chosen; — s.m. (Gr. αἰθηρ), Ether
subs: s.m., The ether, the ninth and greatest of the spheres.

def: part. adj. Bound, tied, made captive; — s.m. Prisoner, captive; — asīr-i-āb-o-gil, Bound or attached to (one's) native soil.
more: s. m. A prisoner, a captive. اسیر آب و گل asīr-i-āb o gil, Attached to oneʼs native land.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَسِیر (asīr), from Arabic أَسِير (ʔasīr).

ety: v.n. and inf. n. of عسر 'to be difficult,' &c.
def: adj. Difficult, hard; — ʻasaruʼl-ʻubūr, Difficult to cross or pass over.
more: adj. Difficult. عسرالعبور aﻌsaru-l-uﻌbūr, Difficult to be passed.

def: s.m. Blessing, &c. = اسيرباد asīr-bād, q.v.
s.m. = اسيرباد asīrbād, q.v.
s.m. Blessing, &c. = اسيرباد asīr-bād, q.v.
s.m. Blessing, benediction; salutation, greeting ( = āsir-bād)
more: n. m. S. आशिस् benediction, वद् to speak. Blessing; benediction. asīrbād denā, asīrbād karnā , v. a. 1. To bless; pronounce a benediction; give a blessing. 2. To salute; greet; make obeisance.
s. m. Benediction, blessing: see آسرباد āsirbād.
subs: (-ko), asīr-bād karnā, v.t. To bestow a blessing (on), pronounce a benediction (on), to bless; to salute, greet; to make obeisance to.

def: s.m. Blessing, benediction; salutation, greeting ( = āsir-bād)
subs: (-ko), asīr-bād karnā, v.t. To bestow a blessing (on), pronounce a benediction (on), to bless; to salute, greet; to make obeisance to.

def: s.f. Imprisonment, captivity.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَسِیرِی (asīrī). By surface analysis, اَسِیر (asīr) + ی (ī).

def: s.f. = اسيس asīs, q.v. s.f. Prayer; blessing, benediction; return of compliments from a (Hindū) superior; reward, prize
subs: (-ko), To bestow or pray for a blessing (upon), to invoke a blessing (upon), to bless; to salute, greet; to make obeisance (to) = asīr-bād denā.

def: s.f. Prayer; blessing, benediction; return of compliments from a (Hindū) superior; reward, prize
more: n. f. S. आशिस् benediction. 1. Blessing; benediction; return of compliments from a (Hindū) superior. 2. Prize; reward; present. Ham laṛkoṅ kī lāo asīs, Laṛke jīweṅ koṛ barīs . Boys' Ṭesū .
subs: (-ko), To bestow or pray for a blessing (upon), to invoke a blessing (upon), to bless; to salute, greet; to make obeisance (to) = asīr-bād denā.

def: s.f. Prayer; blessing, benediction; return of compliments from a (Hindū) superior; reward, prize
more: s. f. Blessing, benediction, return of compliment from a Hindū superiour.
subs: (-ko), To bestow or pray for a blessing (upon), to invoke a blessing (upon), to bless; to salute, greet; to make obeisance (to) = asīr-bād denā.

def: s.f. = آشس āśis, q.v.
s.f. = آشس āśis, q.v.
adj. Untrained, badly-trained, ill-regulated, ill-behaved, unpolite, rude, barbarous; indecent, profligate.
part. adj. = اسشٿ asishta, q.v. adj. Untrained, badly-trained, ill-regulated, ill-behaved, unpolite, rude, barbarous; indecent, profligate.
v.t. = asīs-denā, q.v.s.v. اسيس asīs.
more: v. a. 1. To bless; call blessings for, or on; pray for. 1. Kose jīeṅ, asīse mareṅ . Prov. The cursed ones live; the blessed ones die. 2. Hoë ānaṅd, asīs det hūṅ, achal suhāg ho jāë rī . Wom. Song. Be happy, my blessing take, may your husband live. 3. Pāṅḍe jī, ab deo asīs, Chatye jīweṅ koṭ baris . E. Chak-chaṅdā . ☞ Lines repeated at the school boys' festival of Chak-chandā by the pupils of an indigenous Hindi school, who, with their teacher, make the round of the parents' houses, the teacher receiving some gratuity at each house. 2. To greet; salute; make obeisance to.

def: adj. Black; — s.m. The colour black, the dark half of the month from full to new moon.
adj. Unlucky, inauspicious; not prosperous, unhappy; pernicious, dangerous.
more: s. m. A horse. اسوبت अश्वपति aswa-pati, Lord or master of horse, a horseman: see اشوپت ashwa-pati.

def: adj. = اسو asiv, q.v. adj. Unlucky, inauspicious; not prosperous, unhappy; pernicious, dangerous.
adj. compar. (of ٿقیل) Heavier, heaviest, very heavy.

ety: comp. and super. of ثقيل s̤aqīl
def: adj. Heavier; heaviest, very heavy.
s.m. An army, a force, troop.
more: (from Pers. لشکر) s. m. An army, force, troop. (Plur. عساکر).

def: adj. & s.m. Of or relating to an army, military; — a soldier.
more: adj. Relating to the army, military; s. m. A soldier.

def: s.f. Inactivity, remissness; dulness; tardiness, dilatoriness; slothfulness, laziness, indolence; heaviness, drowsiness, sleepiness; procrastination.
s.f. Inactivity, remissness; dulness; tardiness, dilatoriness; slothfulness, laziness, indolence; heaviness, drowsiness, sleepiness; procrastination.
more: Māṛ. n. f. S. अशक्ति from अ not, शक to be able. 1. Laziness; slothfulness; sluggishness; indolence; remissness; lumpishness; torpor; inaction; inactivity; tardiness; dullness. Phr. The thief of time; castle of indolence. Hāth kī āskat, mūṅh meṅ mūchh . E. Prov. Lazy hands, moustache in mouth. 2. Drowsiness; doziness; sleepiness; nodding ( ūṅgh ). 3. Procrastination; deferring.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit आसक्त (āsakta).

def: v.n. To be inactive; to be lazy, idle, &c.; to be disinclined to work; to be idle; to move slowly, to dawdle, dally, to be dilatory; to take it easy.
v.n. To be inactive; to be lazy, idle, &c.; to be disinclined to work; to be idle; to move slowly, to dawdle, dally, to be dilatory; to take it easy.
more: W. v. n. 1. To be disinclined to work; to be inactive; to do nothing; lie idle; lie by; hang fire; relax; loll. 2. To move slowly; lag; slug; dally; dawdle; loiter; take one's time; idle away; fritter away time; to let alone. Phr. To fold one's arms; not to stir a peg; not to move a finger; to while away the time. Nis, din khānā; kām ko askatānā . E. Prov. He'll eat both night and day, but work he will delay.

def: adj. Inactive, doing nothing, lazy, sluggish, indolent; remiss; slow, tardy, dilatory, backward; drowsy, dozy, sleepy; — s.m. A lazy fellow, idler, sluggard; dawdler, loiterer, &c.
adj. Inactive, doing nothing, lazy, sluggish, indolent; remiss; slow, tardy, dilatory, backward; drowsy, dozy, sleepy; — s.m. A lazy fellow, idler, sluggard; dawdler, loiterer, &c.
more: W. Rus. n. m. A lazy fellow; indolent person; idler; lubbard; sluggard; lubber; do-little; slow-back. Phr. An afternoon man. 1. Askatī girā kūë meṅ. Kahā, "abhī kaun uṭṭhe ." Prov. saying. A sluggard fell into a well. "Who's going to get up yet?" says he. Or thus, Askati, girā kuë meṅ, Kahā "hame ehaë bhalā ." Prov. A sluggard fell into a well, Quoth he, "I'll lie here just as well." 2. Rām nām ko askati, bhojan ko taīyār . Prov. Too slow on Ram to call, to eat too ready all. ās'katī , E. āl'kasī , W. al'hī , Gaṛh. adj. 1. Lazy; sluggish; inactive; doing nothing; slothful; dull; idle; torpid; slack; sluggish; remiss; indolent ( sust ). Phr. With folded arms; with the hands in the pockets. 2. Tardy; slow; late; behind hand; backward.

def: s.f. Bottom, root, origin, base, foundation; original, source; an essential, a fundamental principle; essence; element, principle; chief thing, main point, original or old state or condition; original or primary signification; original copy (of a work or document); a radical letter (as distinguished from an augmentative); reality, fact, truth; race, stock, lineage; a man of good stock, a thorough-bred gentleman; capital, stock-in-trade, principal; — adj. Radical, original, fundamental, &c. = aṣlī, q.v.
more: n. f. 1. Root ( jaṛ ); origin; foundation ( neo ); element ( tat ); source ( sot ); cause ( kāran ); principle; rudiment; basis; seed ( bīj ). 2. Nature; state or form of being; case; condition; character; virtue; property; constitution. 3. Essence ( sat ); essential part ( tant ); pith; point; entity; substance; the root letter (S. धातु ). 4. Truth; fact; reality. 1. Asal kah do, jhūṭ mat bolo . 2. Asal kahe so dārī-jār . E. Prov. Who speaks truth shall get abuse. 5. Lineage; genealogy; family ( kul ); stock; blood; birth; breed; extraction; race; line; descent; pedigree. 1. Us kī asal meṅ faraq hai . There is a stain in his pedigree. 2. Apnī asal pe ā gayā . Prov. His (low) birth has asserted itself. 6. A man of good family; a born gentleman. 1. Asal se khatā nahīṅ, kam-asal se wafā nahīṅ . Prov. Gentility has no flaw, the base-born no fidelity. 2. Asal ke asal hote haiṅ . Prov. Good s…

adj. 1. Essential; vital; fundamental; substantial. 2. Substantive; absolute; positive. 3. Material; important; integral. 4. Principal; chief; capital. 5. Legitimate; born in wedlock. 6. True; real; actual; original. Asal qīmat. Asal asal hai, naqal naqal hai . Prov. The imitation can never come up to the real. 7. True; not false; veracious. 8. Best; fine; exquisite; first rate. 9. Not imaginary; not ideal. asal nām . Real name as distinguished from urf . an alias. asal nafā , Net profit; clear gain. asl o fara . Root and branch, cause and effect.
adv. Personally; (appearing or pleading) in person; himself; in propria persona , in contradistinction to vakālatan (pleading by a vakīl or barrister).
s. f. 1. Root, origin, original, foundation. 2. Lineage. 3. Capital, principal sum, stock in trade.
subs: adv. ( = dar aṣl), In fact, in effect, in reality, really, actually; positively, absolutely
s.m. Real name (in contr. to ʻurf or alias)
s.m. Real or clear gain, net profit
s.f. Root and branch; cause and effect

Aslī aur naqlī chīz meṅ baṛā bal hai . There is a great difference between the real and the imitation. 3. Elementary; rudimental; constituent. 4. Own; native; personal; individual; private. 5. Natural; real; unaffected. aslī kharch , Real cost. aslī kharīd , Prime cost; real cost. aslī zamīn , Original land, exlcusive of subsequent additions, as from alluvial deposits. aslī qīmat , Original, or real value or price. aslī gāoṅ , The original or chief village from which others have branched out, that on which the revenue was originally assessed.

A lame squirrel, and her nest in the sky! (Said of small powers with great ambition). āsmān par thūk'nā , v. n. lit . To spit against the sky. To act improperly or foolishly. Āsmān kā thūkā, mūṅh par āve . Prov. Spit not against heaven. It will fall back on thy face. āsmān par chaṛhānā , v. a. 1. To elevate; exalt; exaggerate; speak Logoṅ ne tārīf kar-kar āsmān par chaṛhā dȳā . 2. To flatter; wheedle; coax ( phuslānā ). āsmān pe dimāg ho'nā , v. n. To be wise in one's own conceit, vain as a peacock, self-conceited, proud, etc. Naë nawāb, āsmān par dimāg . Prov. āsmān pe kheṅchnā, āsmān pe qadam rakhnā, āsmān meṅ pair lagānā , v. n. To exalt oneself; to grow vain or conceited; to affect greatness. Phr. To think …

(comp. of آس ās, A millstone, and مانند Like) s. m. The sky, the firmament, heaven, the celestial orb. آسمان پر قدم رکهنا āsmān par ḳadam rakhnā, To give oneʼs self airs, to aspire, to be vain. آسمان پر کهینچنا āsmān par khainchnā, To be proud, to affect greatness. آسمان سي گرنا āsmān se girnā, To fall from the clouds, is applied, particularly by women, to signify an acquisition that has been made suddenly and unexpectedly, without labour or care, and the value of which is inconsiderable. آسمان قدر āsmān-ḳadr, Vast as the firmament in size.

adj. Heavenly, celestial; from heaven, divine; sky-coloured, azure, cerulean; unexpected, sudden
more: adj. 1. Heavenly; celestial; divine. 2. Azure; cerulean; sky-blue. Āsmānī raṅg . 3. Unexpected; abrupt; sudden. Āsmānī golā ān paṛā. āsmānī ba'lā,—thape'ṛā,—gaz'ab,—go'lā,— tīr , n. A calamity from Heaven; Divine visitation. Girī us pe jo āsmānī balā, Dil us nāznīṅ kā havā ho chalā . Mīr Hasan. āsmānī pilā'nā , v. a. To make one drunk, especially with bhang or toddy. ☞ The metaphor is taken from the feeling of the intoxicated person who seems to himself to be raised up aloft and descending again alternately. Hence the Arabic-Persian expression, falak-sair (Heaven-travel) for bhang and mājūm. āsmānī tīr , n. m. An arrow shot towards the sky; a useless act; an unprofitable action. āsmānī farmā'nī , E. n. f. lit . Heavenly decree. 1. Unforeseen disasters arising from drought or excessive rain, as failure of crops in a bad season. Sukhār dahār āsmānī farmānī hai . E. Drought and flood are He…
adj. Heavenly, celestial, from heaven, sky-coloured, cerulean, azure. آسماني تیروغیره āsmānī tīr wa g̠ẖairah, An arrow, &c. shot in the air.
subs: (-par), āsmānī g̠aẓab ṭūṭnā (-par), To be overtaken by a calamity from heaven, or by divine vengeance

s.m. An arrow shot towards the sky, an arrow shot at random; an arrow shot by an unknown hand; a profitless venture; an useless act

(arising from drought or excessive rain); a phrase formerly used in deeds and leases as a provision against loss or injury arising from calamitous seasons, or unjust exactions of the Government, which, if affecting the zamīndārs, the ryots were compelled to make good.

s.m. Abode, asylum, place of refuge, shelter, retreat, hiding-place; means of defence, protection or security, defence, safeguard, safety, security, surety; support, stay; protector, patron; hope, expectation, trust, reliance, confidence, dependence, assurance; help, means of subsistence
more: n. m. S. आश्रि to resort to; seek refuge. Pālī. asayo . 1. Trust; reliance; dependance; assurance. ( bharosā ). Āsrā Allah, aur ālī Rasūl Āllah kā . Inshā. 2. Confidence; hope; expectation; belief; faith. 1. Abke khetī meṅ āsrā hai . Rus. 2. Apne ḍhig paisā, to parāyā āsrā kaisā . Prov. With money to your cheek, no help you need to seek. 3. Protection; defence; anchor; star; security; safety; surety; safeguard. 4. Asylum; retreat; refuge; screen; ark; covert; hiding place; shelter ( panāh ). Bhāg-kar Dillī meṅ āsrā lȳā . 5. Patron; protector; support; mainstay. 1. Hum apnā āsrā āp hī ko samajhte haiṅ . 2. Hamārā to yeh bachchā hī āsrā hai . 6. Help; means of subsistence ( sahārā ); livelihood. Ham ādh ser āṭe kā āsrā chāhte haiṅ . 7. E. The green or soft layers of wood, in contradistinction to the older and harder ( hīr ). āsrā tak'nā,—ḍhūṅḍh'nā , v. n. To look for help, succour, assist…
s. m. Means of defence or protection, trust, reliance, hope, defence, asylum, retreat, refuge, shelter, abode, house.
subs: (-kā), āsrā johnā (-kā), āsrā ḍhūṅḍhnā -(-kā), To look for help or support (from), seek succour (from), expect the support (of); to look (for), watch (for), be in expectation (of)
(-kā), To lose (one's) hope, to be disappointed in (one's) expectations; to despair
(-ko), To give hope, to raise expectations; to console; to give assurance, inspire confidence, to reassure; to encourage
(-kā), To rest, rely, depend or lean (on), to be dependent (on); to rest or feel assured; to count (on), trust (in), confide (in); to hope, expect
(-kā, or meṅ), To place trust (in); to seek the support (of); to seek the shelter (of), take refuge (in)
(-kā), To be the support, or stay, or prop, or refuge (of).
wety: From Sanskrit आश्रय (āśraya).

s.m. Abode, residence; the abode of a hermit, or of a religious student; hermitage; college, school; — a class or religious order (of which the Hindūs reckon four, referable to the different periods of a Brāhman's life, viz. 1˚ The Brahmāćārī, who devotes his life to religious exercises, aūsterity, and celibacy; 2˚ The Gr̤ihī or Gr̤ihastha, who lives in the world and rears a family; 3˚ The Vānaprastha, who retires from the world with his family and passes his life in devotion in the forest; 4˚ The Bhikshu or Sannyasī, who subsists on alms).
more: n. m. Pālī. assamo , S. आश्रम from आ + श्रम to make effort, perform austerities. 1. Abode, residence (of a religious man). Tapassī jī, āp kā āsram kahāṅ hai? 2. A religious order, called also baran where of the Hindūs reckon four, viz. the Brahmachārī , who devotes his life to religious exercises, austerity and celibacy; 2nd Grīhī or Grihastha , who lives in the world and rears a family; 3rd Vānaprastha , one who retires from the world with his family, and passes his life in devotion in the forest; 4th Bhikshū, Bhichchhū or Sannyāsī , one who lives on alms. Ajī, tum kaun āsram ho?

s. m. 1. Abode, residence. 2. A religious order (of which there are four kinds referable to the different periods of life: as, 1st. That of the ब्रह्मचारी brahmachārī, or student; 2d. That of the गृहस्थ gr̤ihastha, or householder; 3d. That of the वानप्रस्थ vānaprastha, or anchorite: and 4thly. That of the भिक्षु bhiksh̤u, or beggar).
